description: Build shared integration test infrastructure: TestWorkdir (isolated temp directory with symlinked Foundry project), TrebRunner (CLI subprocess wrapper), AnvilNode (managed Anvil instance), and TestContext (composition struct). Eliminates duplicated test setup code across CLI integration tests.

userStories.title: TestWorkdir with Lightweight Workspace Creation
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a TestWorkdir struct that creates isolated temp directories by symlinking immutable deps and copying mutable files from a fixture directory, so that each test gets a fast, independent workspace.
acceptanceCriteria: crates/treb-cli/tests/framework/mod.rs exists and declares sub-modules: workdir, runner, anvil_node, context
TestWorkdir::new() creates a temp dir with symlinks for lib/, test/, .gitignore, remappings.txt from the fixture
TestWorkdir::new() recursively copies foundry.toml, treb.toml, src/, script/ from the fixture
.treb/ directory is created automatically in the temp dir
Missing fixture files/dirs are silently skipped (no error if fixture has no test/ dir)
TREB_TEST_SKIP_CLEANUP=1 preserves the temp dir and prints its path to stderr
Minimal project fixture exists at tests/fixtures/minimal-project/ with foundry.toml, src/Counter.sol, script/Deploy.s.sol
Unit test verifies symlinks are symlinks (not copies) and copies are independent files
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: Port Rewrite Utility for foundry.toml
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a port_rewrite_foundry_toml() function that replaces hardcoded localhost RPC URLs in foundry.toml with test-specific port numbers, so that parallel tests can each use their own Anvil instance without port conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: port_rewrite_foundry_toml() reads and rewrites foundry.toml in the workdir
Replacement handles localhost:8545, 127.0.0.1:8545, and http://localhost:8545 patterns plus port 9545 variants
Multiple rewrites can be applied in a single call (e.g., mainnet on one port, testnet on another)
port_rewrite_foundry_toml_single() convenience function rewrites all 8545/9545 to a given port
Function returns an error if foundry.toml doesn't exist in the workdir
Unit test verifies port substitution with a sample foundry.toml content
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: TrebRunner CLI Wrapper
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a TrebRunner struct that wraps assert_cmd::Command with project defaults (workdir, --non-interactive, 60s timeout, debug output), so that CLI invocations in tests are consistent and observable.
acceptanceCriteria: TrebRunner::new() stores the workdir path
TrebRunner::run() invokes the treb-cli binary with --non-interactive injected as the first argument
Working directory is set to the workdir for every invocation
Timeout is set to 60 seconds on all invocations
TREB_TEST_DEBUG=1 prints binary path, args, cwd, and exit code to stderr
TrebRunner::run_with_env() passes additional env vars to the subprocess
Test verifies treb version runs successfully via TrebRunner
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: AnvilNode Wrapper
userStories.description: As a test author, I want an AnvilNode struct wrapping treb_forge::AnvilConfig that provides spawn/accessors/cleanup for Anvil instances, so that tests can easily manage Anvil nodes with OS-assigned ports.
acceptanceCriteria: AnvilNode::spawn() creates an Anvil instance on an OS-assigned port (port 0)
port(), chain_id(), and rpc_url() return correct values from the running instance
AnvilNode::spawn_with_config() accepts custom AnvilConfig settings
Dropping AnvilNode frees the port (underlying AnvilInstance abort handles fire)
Async test verifies spawn, port accessibility, and clean drop
cargo check --workspace passes with no errors

userStories.title: TestContext Composition and Smoke Test
userStories.description: As a test author, I want a TestContext struct composing TestWorkdir, TrebRunner, and optional AnvilNode instances, plus smoke tests proving the full pipeline works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: TestContext::new() creates a workdir and runner from a fixture directory
TestContext::with_anvil() registers a named node and rewrites foundry.toml ports
TestContext::run() and run_with_env() delegate to TrebRunner correctly
Smoke test: treb version succeeds via TestContext
Smoke test: treb init creates .treb/ directory structure in the workdir
Async smoke test: Anvil node is reachable and CLI can interact with it
TREB_TEST_DEBUG=1 and TREB_TEST_SKIP_CLEANUP=1 work correctly during smoke tests
cargo test --workspace passes with all existing and new tests green
